title: Polypodium anceps
taxonomy: Polypodium anceps is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody ferns. This family is part of the larger group known as the Polypodiales, which consists of about 1,200 species of ferns. The Polypodiaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes. Polypodium anceps is native to tropical regions of Asia and Australia. It is typically found in shaded, humid areas with rich soil.
uses: Polypodium anceps has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Pacific Islands, where it is believed to possess various health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing pain and swelling. In some cultures, it is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its purported expectorant properties. Additionally, the plant is believed to have antiseptic properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds and infections. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Polypodium anceps.

intro: Polypodium anceps, also known as Polypodium leucotomos var. leucotomos or Polypodium leucotomos, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against sun damage, improve skin health, and support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Polypodium anceps include flavonoids, alkaloids, and sesquiterpenoids, such as leucocyanidin and quercetin, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Polypodium anceps is often used to make tinctures, capsules, and topical creams, as well as being used in traditional remedies such as 'Polypodium leucotomos extract' commonly known as ' Solar Decorticator'
